en.extract:
The Sulawesi cuckoo (Cuculus crassirostris) is a species of cuckoo in the family Cuculidae. It is often known as the Sulawesi hawk-cuckoo but appears not to be related to the other hawk-cuckoos. It is endemic to Sulawesi Island in Indonesia. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

[[File:Sulawesi cuckoo calling.jpg|thumb|]]
The '''Sulawesi cuckoo''' ('''''Cuculus crassirostris''''') is a species of [[cuckoo]] in the family [[Cuculidae]]. It is often known as the '''Sulawesi hawk-cuckoo''' but appears not to be related to the other hawk-cuckoos.<ref>Payne, Robert B. (2005) ''The Cuckoos'', Oxford University Press.</ref> It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Sulawesi]] Island in [[Indonesia]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.
